buggy_code:
import pandas as pd

df = pd.read_csv('prices.csv', parse_dates=['date'], index_col='date')

# Chained indexing — may not modify original DataFrame
df[df['volume'] > 1000000]['signal'] = 1
df[df['volume'] <= 1000000]['signal'] = 0

# df['signal'] may still be all NaN
print(df['signal'].value_counts())
clean_code:
import pandas as pd

df = pd.read_csv('prices.csv', parse_dates=['date'], index_col='date')

# Use .loc to avoid chained indexing
df['signal'] = 0  # Initialize first
df.loc[df['volume'] > 1000000, 'signal'] = 1

print(df['signal'].value_counts())
description: Chained indexing used to assign values — silently fails to modify the original DataFrame.
explanation: df[condition]['column'] = value creates a temporary copy of the filtered rows and assigns to that copy, not the original. The original DataFrame is unchanged. This is a SettingWithCopyWarning in pandas and a silent data error — the signal column never gets set, producing wrong strategy behavior with no error message.
suggested_fix: Always use .loc[condition, column] = value for conditional assignment to avoid chained indexing.

issues.title: Chained indexing silently fails to assign values
